mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic activity, which increases the demand for capital and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: If GDPC1 grows > 2% annualized but 10-year yield falls more than 50% of the time over 60-trading-day windows, or if the correlation between GDPC1 and yield changes is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.2) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crosses above 4.5%, it signals economic deterioration and investors seek safe-haven Treasuries, driving the 10-year yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses 4.5%, the 10-year yield rises in more than 60% of instances over a 20-day horizon, or the average yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a recession regime defined by year-over-year decline in GDPC1, a fall in industrial production (INDPRO) intensifies recession fears and flight-to-quality, causing the 10-year yield to decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during recession regimes, a 1% decline in INDPRO is associated with a positive yield change more than 30% of the time, or the average yield change is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising and the Federal Reserve (FEDFUNDS) is slow to respond, real interest rates fall and breakeven inflation expectations rise, pushing nominal 10-year yields upward over the next 20 trading days. The joint behavior of higher inflation with stable or falling rates is key.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (CPIAUCSL change * -FEDFUNDS change) does not show a significant positive coefficient in predicting 20-day yield changes (p-value < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal robust supply-side growth, which tightens resource utilization and pushes up long-term yields as the market prices in future capacity constraints.
failure_condition: If the rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO and 10-year yield changes is not above 0.2, or if the average yield change in months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) drop below 1,000 thousand units, it indicates a severe downturn in the housing sector that often precedes a broader recession, causing investors to seek safe-haven Treasury securities and lowering the 10-year yield over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: If after HOUST falls below 1000, the 10-year yield rises in more than 40% of 60-day windows, or if the decline in yield is less than 20 bps on average.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, defined as FEDFUNDS increasing over at least three consecutive meetings, the market anticipates further rate hikes, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the subsequent 20 trading days as term premiums increase.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS increases over 3 months)
failure_condition: If during tightening cycles defined by FEDFUNDS increases, the 10-year yield falls in more than 50% of 20-day periods, or the average yield change is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) produce a strong signal of aggregate demand outpacing supply, which accelerates inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next week (5 trading days).
failure_condition: If the cross-product of RSAFS and INDPRO monthly changes does not show a statistically significant positive relationship with 5-day yield changes (t-stat > 1.96) over a 2-year period.
